method: Firecrawl search+scrape: Cinevva 2026 guide, OpenReplay JS engines, Utsubo Three/Babylon/PlayCanvas, Godot web docs

rationale: Already shipping four playable modes in single-file HTML (~30KB each + CDN Three.js ~168KB gzip)
Best fit for quivly.ai embed: tiny load, URL-shareable, no install
Largest ecosystem + best AI/vibe-coding support (Cursor, Claude, llms.txt)
React Three Fiber path if game moves into Next.js app later
WebGPU ready (r171+) with WebGL2 fallback — Safari 26 closed last holdout in 2025

doNotUseForShip.reason: No native HTML5 export; Pixel Streaming = server cost + latency per user
~8MB+ empty build, slow load — bad for marketing embeds
~9MB gzipped, Safari issues, C# can't export to web — overkill for 90s arcade

2dArcade.note: PagerTron-style 2D; Phaser = biggest community, Defold = smallest builds (~1.14MB)

3dRunnerShooter.note: Three = flexible + tiny; Babylon = batteries-included physics/audio/XR

3dWithEditor.note: Cloud editor, ~1-2MB runtime, mature WebGPU — best if artists join

comparison.bestFor: Embeds, marketing games, React sites, AI-assisted dev
Indie 2D/3D with editor, not marketing embeds
High-fidelity viz with server budget — not arcade

renewal-rush.html.fit: good — could migrate to Phaser if 2D expands
